sources.notes: Color edges/vertices so gcd constraints match maxflow/mincut values.
Official editorial uses max-flow/min-cut structure to construct a coloring satisfying all gcd constraints.

annotation.summary: Construct a coloring that realizes required GCD values encoded by maxflow constraints.
annotation.constraints: The graph size is moderate enough for repeated maxflow or mincut-style construction.
annotation.core_idea: Model each prime/divisor layer independently. For a fixed layer, vertices/edges requiring divisibility form source/sink constraints, and a feasible separation is exactly a min-cut/max-flow condition. Solving the layered cut instances gives binary decisions for whether each object contains the divisor; combining all layers reconstructs colors whose gcds match the required values.
annotation.complexity: Polynomial maxflow over divisor layers; roughly O(L * flow(n,m)).
tricks: decompose gcd constraints by divisor/prime layer
use min-cut as a 0/1 coloring certificate
combine layers into final numeric colors

solution_variants.name: layered-mincut-coloring
solution_variants.summary: For each divisor layer, solve a maxflow/mincut feasibility problem and combine the resulting binary layers into colors.

tags.evidence: The official solution is explicitly based on maxflow/mincut feasibility.
The color constraints are GCD constraints and are decomposed by divisor layers.
The solution must output an actual coloring assembled from cut decisions.
